LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Glowing_One_Zone_Forager.d: [PAWN_nameDef] survived by gathering edible plants and fungi from the most irradiated places, where only the glowing dare tread.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Glowing_One_Zone_Guide.des: [PAWN_nameDef] led non-ghouls through dangerous glowing zones, charging a hefty fee for [PAWN_possessive] expertise and protection.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Glowing_One_Zone_Herbalist: [PAWN_nameDef] learned to identify and use rare, mutated plants found only in radioactive zones, brewing potent medicines and poisons.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Glowing_One_Zone_Hermit.de: [PAWN_nameDef] lived alone in the heart of a radioactive wasteland, rarely seeing another soul. [PAWN_pronoun] is self-reliant and wary of outsiders.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Glowing_One_Zone_Outlaw.de: Banished from settlements, [PAWN_nameDef] survived by raiding travelers and hiding in radioactive ruins where few would follow.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Glowing_One_Zone_Scavenger: [PAWN_nameDef] braved the most radioactive ruins to recover valuable salvage, risking [PAWN_possessive] life for rare technology and supplies.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Wastelander_Post_war_Bodyg: [PAWN_nameDef] made a living by protecting affluent customers from the dangers of the wasteland. [PAWN_pronoun] protected wealthy caravaners, important officials from everything and everyone that would harm them, as long as they paid of course. While it may have been less lethal than working as a mercenary, it was still dangerous and [PAWN_nameDef] had the scars to prove it.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Wastelander_Post_war_Under: Life in the wasteland was often brutal, arduous and short. Some of the larger settlements employed an undertaker. [PAWN_nameDef] was one of those undertakers and [PAWN_possessive] job was to dig graves and bury those whose lives were claimed by raiders, radiation, mutated beasts and, occasionally, old age.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Wastelander_Pre_war_Gourme: Just the thought of eating radroaches or molerats made [PAWN_nameDef] shiver with disgust. [PAWN_pronoun] was what some called an old-world gourmet and preferred to dine on ancient rations of canned meat, mac and cheese and Salisbury steak. While most members of [PAWN_possessive] community only shook their heads at [PAWN_possessive] behaviour, none could argue that [PAWN_pronoun] was indeed a good cook.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Advisor.description: An esteemed and wise warrior, [PAWN_nameDef] was an advisor to the tribe’s leader. [PAWN_pronoun] was unusually level-headed for a Khan and advised against rushed and overly violent actions, which some of the younger warriors saw as a sign of weakness. Nevertheless, [PAWN_pronoun] was a more than capable warrior and always acted with the wellbeing of the tribe in mind.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Advisor.title: Khan Advisor
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Arena_Fighter.descrip: Most Great Khan camps featured a prominent arena, usually in the centre of it. These were used to carry out the brutal initiation ritual of the tribe but were also used for “recreational” purposes. [PAWN_nameDef] was one of the most eager combatants of the arena and made a small fortune by betting on [PAWN_objective]self. While [PAWN_pronoun] wasn’t a very good shot, few Khans could match [PAWN_objective] in close combat.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Arena_Fighter.title: Khan Arena Fighter
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Armorer.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was an armorer with the Khans and was tasked with maintaining the tribe’s weapons and armor. The Khans were a poor tribe and had little access to high-end weapons or armor, but [PAWN_nameDef] was resourceful and managed to create descent armor from hides and scraps of scavenged metal.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Armorer.title: Khan Armorer
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Born_Khan.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was born into the raider tribe of the Great Khans. While [PAWN_pronoun] might not have had much of a formalized education, [PAWN_pronoun] learned much about the land the gang resided on.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Born_Khan.title: Born Khan
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Cave_Dweller.descript: While most Khans preferred to live in yurts, many built their camps near caves. While this might seem primitive, even for the Khans, natural caves made easily defendable homes and were usually much less prone to collapse compared to pre-war ruins. [PAWN_nameDef] grew up in and around an extensive cave system and helped expand it, if needed.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Cave_Dweller.title: Khan Cave Dweller
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Chem_Maker.descriptio: [PAWN_nameDef] was a gifted chemist and an important member of [PAWN_possessive] tribe, as [PAWN_possessive] chems proved to be a reliable source of income for the otherwise poor and isolated group. While most Khans didn’t concern themselves with intellectual pursuits, [PAWN_nameDef] was always on the lookout for old books to learn new tricks to perfect [PAWN_possessive] trade.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Chem_Maker.title: Khan Chem Maker
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Chem_Runner.descripti: [PAWN_nameDef] was one of the few Khans of [PAWN_possessive] tribe who often spent extended periods of time far away from the camp. [PAWN_pronoun] was a chem runner, tasked with delivering the tribe’s products to their clients, be they raiders, doctors or caravan outfits. Due to the value of [PAWN_possessive] goods, [PAWN_nameDef] had to be shrewd and cunning to evade rival chem dealers, as well as desperate addicts looking for a fix.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Chem_Runner.title: Chem Runner
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Child_Prodigy.descrip: Since the Khans rarely had access to high-tech workshops, children like [PAWN_nameDef] who had a natural aptitude maintaining machines and weaponry were usually fostered and trained by the tribe’s armourers.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Child_Prodigy.title: Khan Child Prodigy
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Daredevil.description: The Great Khans relaxed attitude towards their offspring’s upbringing often led to risky or outright dangerous games and activities the children participated in. [PAWN_nameDef] was known as one of the most daring children of [PAWN_possessive] tribe. [PAWN_pronoun] greatly enjoyed taunting tribe’s bighorners and similar games of questionable pedagogic value.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Daredevil.title: Khan Daredevil
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Educated_Khan.descrip: [PAWN_nameDef] was one of the few Khan children who were educated by a group of traveling Followers of the Apocalypse. The followers taught [PAWN_objective] how to read and write as well as some basics of natural sciences and humanities. While this might not have counted for much in other societies, it made [PAWN_nameDef] one of the most well-read members of the tribe.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Educated_Khan.title: Educated Khan
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Firekeeper.descriptio: [PAWN_nameDef] never was among the most skilled fighters in the Khans. [PAWN_pronoun] was tasked with keeping the fires in the tribe’s camp going, looking after the wounded and sick and preparing the tribe’s meals. As the Khans had little access to advanced medicine, [PAWN_nameDef] learned to make do with the herbs that grew around the camp.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Firekeeper.title: Khan Firekeeper
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Forager.description: Most Great Khan encampments were located in barren and isolated areas where agriculture proved to be difficult. However, many plants grew even in the most desolate places and many of them were edible or otherwise useful to the tribe. [PAWN_nameDef] was tasked with finding and gathering these plants.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Forager.title: Khan Forager
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Foundling.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was an abandoned child found and taken in by a group of Khans. While [PAWN_pronoun] might not have been born a Khan, [PAWN_pronoun] certainly grew up to become one.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Foundling.title: Khan Foundling
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Guard.description: As one of the tribe’s most elite fighters, [PAWN_nameDef] was assigned as a personal guard to Papa Khan, the Khan’s undisputed leader. [PAWN_pronoun] took [PAWN_possessive] this duty extremely serious, despite the fact that only a madman would dare to attack Papa Khan amidst a Khan camp. However, [PAWN_possessive] privileged position made [PAWN_objective] somewhat haughty towards the other members of the tribe and [PAWN_pronoun] was usually not well-liked.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Guard.title: Khan Guard
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Herder.description: As the Khans were expelled from the relatively fertile lands of California, they had to adapt to new, harsher environments where agriculture was rarely possible and was soon replaced entirely by pastoralism. [PAWN_nameDef] was a herder among the Khans, watching the tribes bighorners and brahmin on which they greatly depended for food, hides and dung for fuel and chem production. It wasn’t the most honourable task, but [PAWN_nameDef] was content to do [PAWN_possessive] part.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Herder.title: Khan Herder
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Hunter.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was a hunter with the Great Khans. Hunting the usually very dangerous beasts of the wasteland was seen as the second most respected task, just short of being a warrior. [PAWN_nameDef] was a skilled shot and knew how to live off the land for extended periods of time while [PAWN_pronoun] prowled through the wasteland on the lookout for worthwhile quarry to feed [PAWN_possessive] tribe.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Hunter.title: Khan Hunter
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Idealistic_Khan_Young: [PAWN_nameDef] was born long after the glory days of the Great Khans and, like many other, longed for the times when the wasteland trembled in fear before them. [PAWN_pronoun] sought to claim the tribe’s legacy, no matter the cost.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Labourer.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was a member of the Great Khans but showed little aptitude for the tribe’s preferred activities, namely fighting, hunting and the production and distribution of drugs. Instead, [PAWN_pronoun] was put to work in areas other Khans couldn’t or wouldn’t do.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Labourer.title: Khan Labourer
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Legend_Keeper.descrip: While the Khans never were among the most culturally sophisticated people, they greatly valued their legends. [PAWN_nameDef] was a keeper of those legends and decorated walls and rocks near their camp with crude but colourful murals depicting the stories of mighty warriors, great victories and devastating losses of [PAWN_possessive] tribe.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Legend_Keeper.title: Khan Legend Keeper
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Mercenary.description: Due to the tribe’s notorious shortage of caps, some sought work outside of the camp as mercenaries, to send part of the caps back home. [PAWN_nameDef] was one of them and the Khan’s fearsome reputation as brutal savages meant there was no shortage of work, albeit [PAWN_pronoun] often ended up getting tricked by [PAWN_possessive] employers.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Mercenary.title: Khan Mercenary
Not all Khans were well-suited as parents. Many were avid users of narcotics and neglected their children. [PAWN_nameDef] parents were usually drunk or high and even when not cared little about their offspring. [PAWN_pronoun] had to fend for [PAWN_objective]self from a very young age and consequently disliked to depend on others even as an adult.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Negotiator.descriptio: While they essentially still were raiders, the Khans were always on the lookout for allies to aid them in their struggle against the NCR. [PAWN_nameDef] was well-spoken and refined (for a Khan anyway) and was often tasked with negotiations, be they military alliances or trade agreements.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Negotiator.title: Khan Negotiator
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Poet.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was an oddity among the Great Khans, as [PAWN_pronoun] showed little interest in combat and the telling of stories about great deeds. Instead, [PAWN_pronoun] preferred to write poetry of debatable quality, something that made [PAWN_objective] a preferred target to the other Khans’ mockery.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Poet.title: Khan Poet
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Raid_Survivor.descrip: [PAWN_nameDef] was one of the few survivors of a Great Khan raid. While the Khans were aptly known as savage fighters, they usually did not kill children. [PAWN_nameDef] was spared and instead taken into the tribe as one of their own. [PAWN_pronoun] was well accepted but nevertheless harboured a grudge against those who murdered [PAWN_possessive] family.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Raid_Survivor.title: Raid Survivor
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Ritualist.description: While the Great Khans were by no means religious people, there were rituals and traditions they held dear. [PAWN_nameDef] was charged with overseeing the tribe’s brutal rites of initiation and patching up those who survived it, as well as conducting ancestor worship rituals, usually composed of commemorative feasts.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Ritualist.title: Khan Ritualist
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Roaming_Khan_Kid.desc: As a Child with the Khans, [PAWN_nameDef] spent most of [PAWN_possessive] time roaming through the lands surrounding their camps and [PAWN_pronoun] learned much about the plants and beasts that inhabited the wasteland from the elders.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Ruffian.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was well-suited for the life of a Khan. [PAWN_pronoun] was prone to rowdy behaviour and quickly became somewhat of a leader for the tribe’s children. What [PAWN_pronoun] lacked in intellect, [PAWN_pronoun] made up for with cunning and many foresaw [PAWN_objective] a future as a great warrior.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Ruffian.title: Khan Ruffian
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Runt.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was born small and weak. Few Khan Children with these attributes survive their early childhood, either because of the harsh living conditions or because they are killed outright since a weak child would not survive the brutal initiation rites anyway. [PAWN_nameDef], however, endured and even prospered, a testament to [PAWN_possessive] tenacity.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Runt.title: Khan Runt
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Savage.description: Brawny and simple-minded, [PAWN_nameDef] was a walking stereotype of a Khan. [PAWN_pronoun] enjoyed brawls, drinking, telling exaggerated stories and little else. While [PAWN_pronoun] might not have been the brightest person to ever wander the wasteland, [PAWN_pronoun] sure could punch hard.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Savage.title: Khan Savage
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Scout.description: The Great Khans had a propensity for paranoia, which was no surprise considering their recent history. [PAWN_nameDef] served [PAWN_possessive] tribe as a scout and was permanently on the lookout for potential enemies, such as NCR patrols, around the camp. While [PAWN_pronoun] was an adequate fighter, [PAWN_pronoun] knew very well that the Khans lacked both numbers and training to put up a real fight against the NCR.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Scout.title: Khan Scout
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Storyteller.descripti: Among the few things the Khans enjoyed greatly that weren’t linked to violence or intoxication was the telling of often exaggerated stories. While most Khans did this to some degree, [PAWN_nameDef] made an artform of it. [PAWN_pronoun] was so good at it that few of [PAWN_possessive] tribespeople cared about the fact that most of them weren’t even remotely based on true events.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Storyteller.title: Khan Storyteller
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Trader.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was a Khan and an unusual one at that, since [PAWN_pronoun] was a trader. Bartering for things instead of simply taking them by force became necessary, since the tribe tried to avoid the attention and ire of the NCR. The tribe had few things to spare, but the hides of animals usually fetched a good enough price for [PAWN_nameDef] to buy much needed goods usually water, ammo and foodstuffs.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Trader.title: Khan Trader
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Tribal_Survivor.descr: As a child [PAWN_nameDef] was a survivor of a tribe eradicated by a ruthless group of raiders. [PAWN_pronoun] was found and taken in by a group of Khans who happened upon the smouldering ruins of [PAWN_possessive] village. [PAWN_possessive] tribal knowledge about the plants and animals of the wasteland served the Khans well.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Tribal_Survivor.title: Tribal Survivor
Tribal Survivor
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Vengeful_Survivor.des: The Khans’ history was filled with tragedies and disasters, most importantly the massacre at Bitter Springs. Some Khans tried to suppress these memories with drugs and booze while others, among them [PAWN_nameDef], became vicious killers with no goal or long-term plan but vengeance. While most Khans went on living as well as they could, [PAWN_possessive] thoughts were with the many dead the tribe had to mourn and [PAWN_nameDef] was determined to answer in kind.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Visionary.description: While many Khans were dissatisfied with the tribe’s current situation, few had ideas on how to change it. [PAWN_nameDef] was one of those Khans who dreamed of greatness. [PAWN_pronoun] wanted to lead them out of their desolate hideouts and into the wilderness of the north to carve out an empire of their own. Of course, not all of the notoriously stubborn Khans were enthusiastic about [PAWN_possessive] ideas.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Visionary.title: Khan Visionary
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_War_Party_Leader.desc: As one of the tribe’s most capable fighters, [PAWN_nameDef] was leading the war parties which often roamed the camp’s surroundings on the lookout for caravans to waylay or promising ruins to scavenge. [PAWN_pronoun] was beloved by the fighting men and women of the tribe, not only because of [PAWN_possessive] valour, but for [PAWN_possessive] genuine concern and care for all that fought by [PAWN_possessive] side.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Warrior.description: Tough and resilient, [PAWN_nameDef] was a warrior with the Great Khans. [PAWN_pronoun] often joined the tribe’s war parties on their raids through the wasteland and hoped to bring back valuables and much needed supplies for the tribe, either scavenged from ruins or taken off of some unfortunate caravan.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Warrior.title: Khan Warrior
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Warriors_Child.descri: [PAWN_nameDef] was the child of one of the tribe’s greatest warriors. [PAWN_pronoun] tried to emulate [PAWN_possessive] parent, much to the pride of [PAWN_possessive] progenitor.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Warriors_Child.title: Warrior’s Child
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Young_Herder.descript: The Khans never were among the most affluent groups of the wasteland. As such even the younger children usually had to help the tribe survive in the harsh wastes they called home. [PAWN_nameDef] assisted the tribe’s herders and looked after the bighorners and brahmins which they greatly depended on.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Young_Herder.title: Young Herder
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Yurt_Maker.descriptio: Many Khans eschewed living in houses and preferred traditional tents called yurts or gers which, despite their size, could be quickly disassembled and transported in case of relocation. [PAWN_nameDef] was an expert at tailoring these tents from animal hides and scavenged fabrics. Their sturdiness was testament to [PAWN_possessive] skills and [PAWN_pronoun] was greatly valued by [PAWN_possessive] tribe.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_Khan_Yurt_Maker.title: Yurt Maker
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Anders_Adult.bas: [PAWN_nameDef] served the Great Khans as a loyal soldier, fighting in raids and defending the camp. [PAWN_pronoun] was still relatively young and eager to prove [PAWN_pronoun] could be as fierce as the veteran warriors. [PAWN_pronoun] idolized Papa Khan and the other leaders, seeing them as the family [PAWN_pronoun] never had. [PAWN_possessive] loyalty to the tribe was absolute.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Anders_Child.bas: [PAWN_nameDef] was orphaned young and survived on the streets until the Great Khans recruited [PAWN_objective]. The tribe gave [PAWN_objective] purpose, training, and a new family. [PAWN_pronoun] eagerly embraced Khan culture, grateful for a place to belong and determined to prove [PAWN_possessive] worth.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Diane_Adult.base: [PAWN_nameDef] became one of the Great Khans' master chem-makers and dealers. [PAWN_pronoun] produced high-quality psycho, jet, and other chems that kept caps flowing into the tribe. [PAWN_pronoun] understood the business side of the trade, negotiating with buyers and maintaining supply chains. Some questioned the morality of spreading addiction, but [PAWN_pronoun] saw it as survival - the Khans had little else to trade.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Diane_Child.base: [PAWN_nameDef] showed an early aptitude for mixing chemicals and understanding reactions. The Khan chem-makers noticed [PAWN_possessive] talent and began teaching [PAWN_objective] the secrets of their trade. [PAWN_pronoun] learned that chems weren't just products - they were the tribe's primary source of income and survival.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Jack_Adult.baseD: [PAWN_nameDef] became the Great Khans' primary armorer, maintaining weapons and crafting armor from whatever materials could be scavenged. [PAWN_pronoun] took pride in [PAWN_possessive] work, knowing that quality gear could mean the difference between life and death for [PAWN_possessive] fellow Khans. [PAWN_pronoun] dreamed of obtaining better tools and materials to improve the tribe's equipment.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Jack_Adult.title: Khan Armorer
Armorer
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Jack_Child.baseD: [PAWN_nameDef] learned to work with metal and machinery from a young age. While other Khan children played at being raiders, [PAWN_pronoun] was fascinated by how things worked. [PAWN_pronoun] collected scrap and learned to turn it into useful items, developing skills that would prove valuable to the tribe.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Jack_Child.title: Scavenger's Apprentice
Apprentice
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Jerry_Adult.base: [PAWN_nameDef] became a recruiter for the Great Khans, seeking out potential members among wasteland outcasts and troublemakers. [PAWN_pronoun] had a gift for identifying those who would thrive in Khan culture and convincing them to join. [PAWN_pronoun] wasn't the best fighter, but [PAWN_possessive] ability to bring in fresh blood made [PAWN_objective] valuable. Some called [PAWN_objective] "Jerry the Punk" for [PAWN_possessive] cocky attitude.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Jerry_Child.base: [PAWN_nameDef] wasn't born into the Khans - [PAWN_pronoun] was a wasteland kid who survived through cunning and opportunism. [PAWN_pronoun] learned to talk [PAWN_possessive] way out of trouble and to spot weaknesses in others. When the Khans found [PAWN_objective], [PAWN_pronoun] saw a chance for something better than living hand to mouth.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Jessup_Adult.bas: [PAWN_nameDef] became a Great Khan raider, participating in operations across the Mojave. [PAWN_pronoun] wasn't the most skilled fighter, but [PAWN_pronoun] was reliable and followed orders. [PAWN_pronoun] sometimes questioned whether the raiding lifestyle was sustainable, but kept those doubts to [PAWN_pronoun]self. [PAWN_pronoun] owed the Khans for taking [PAWN_objective] in when [PAWN_pronoun] had nothing.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Jessup_Child.bas: [PAWN_nameDef] spent [PAWN_possessive] youth wandering the wasteland with [PAWN_possessive] family, never staying in one place long. [PAWN_pronoun] learned to navigate dangerous terrain and read situations quickly. When [PAWN_possessive] family was killed by raiders, the Khans took [PAWN_objective] in.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Karl_Adult.baseD: [PAWN_nameDef] served as a guard for the Great Khans, protecting the camp and standing watch against threats. [PAWN_pronoun] took [PAWN_possessive] duties seriously, understanding that vigilance kept the tribe safe. [PAWN_pronoun] was loyal to Papa Khan and followed orders without question, embodying the traditional Khan values of strength and loyalty.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Karl_Adult.title: Khan Guard
Guard
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Karl_Child.baseD: [PAWN_nameDef] was born and raised in the Great Khans, learning their ways from birth. [PAWN_pronoun] grew up hearing stories of past raids and victories, idolizing the warriors who fought the NCR. [PAWN_pronoun] trained hard to prove [PAWN_pronoun] could be just as tough as the legends.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Karl_Child.title: Khan Born
Khan Born
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Melissa_Adult.ba: [PAWN_nameDef] became one of the Great Khans' fiercest warriors, driven by the need for revenge against the NCR. [PAWN_pronoun] trained relentlessly, becoming skilled with multiple weapons. [PAWN_possessive] hatred ran so deep that [PAWN_pronoun] opposed any suggestion of peace or cooperation with the bear. For [PAWN_objective], there could be no forgiveness for Bitter Springs.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Melissa_Child.ba: [PAWN_nameDef] survived the Bitter Springs Massacre as a teenager. [PAWN_pronoun] watched NCR troopers gun down [PAWN_possessive] friends and family members who were fleeing toward safety. The trauma of that day burned itself into [PAWN_possessive] soul, creating an unquenchable hatred for the NCR and everyone who wore the bear.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Oscar_Adult.base: [PAWN_nameDef] became a respected warrior among the Great Khans. [PAWN_pronoun] participated in numerous raids and battles, earning [PAWN_possessive] place through combat prowess. [PAWN_pronoun] believed in the old ways - that the strong should take what they need and the weak deserved their fate. [PAWN_pronoun] remained skeptical of softer approaches to survival.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Oscar_Child.base: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up learning the raider lifestyle - taking what you need and surviving by strength. [PAWN_pronoun] participated in [PAWN_possessive] first raid as a young teenager, learning that violence was sometimes necessary for survival. The Khan way of life felt natural to [PAWN_objective].
[PAWN_nameDef] became Papa Khan, leader of the Great Khans. [PAWN_pronoun] led the tribe after their devastating defeat at Bitter Springs, where NCR forces massacred women, children, and elders. This tragedy hardened [PAWN_objective], making [PAWN_objective] bitter toward the NCR and deeply protective of what remained of [PAWN_possessive] people. [PAWN_pronoun] struggles between the Khan traditions of raiding and violence, and the need to ensure the tribe's survival in a changing wasteland.
[PAWN_nameDef] was born into the leadership of the Great Khans, son of a previous Khan. [PAWN_pronoun] grew up hearing stories of past glories and devastating defeats at the hands of the NCR. From childhood, [PAWN_pronoun] was trained to be a warrior and leader, learning the Khan way of strength, freedom, and loyalty to the tribe above all else.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Regis_Adult.base: [PAWN_nameDef] became an advisor to Papa Khan, serving as a voice of reason and long-term thinking. [PAWN_pronoun] witnessed the Bitter Springs Massacre and understood that continuing the old ways would only lead to extinction. [PAWN_pronoun] advocated for evolution - maintaining Khan identity while adapting to survive. Some saw [PAWN_objective] as weak, but [PAWN_pronoun] understood that true strength meant ensuring the tribe's future.
LanguageData.FCP_Character_Backstory_Regis_Child.base: [PAWN_nameDef] was always more contemplative than the average Khan youth. While other children gloried in tales of battle, [PAWN_pronoun] paid attention to why battles were won or lost. [PAWN_pronoun] learned to read and studied what few books the Khans possessed, understanding that brains could be as valuable as brawn.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Academics_Child.descri: [PAWN_nameDef]’s parents were scientists working for the Office of Science and Industry, OSI for short. Accordingly, they thought much of higher education and ensured that their offspring was instructed in many scientific fields. While [PAWN_pronoun] was a gifted student, [PAWN_pronoun] never was much of an artist.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Academics_Child.title: Academic’s Child
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Ambassador.description: As the NCR tried to emulate pre-war society, they adapted the tradition of sending ambassadors to tribes and civilisations, deemed worthy of their attention. [PAWN_nameDef] was one of those ambassadors, well versed in statesmanship and shaking hands with both tribal chiefs and foreign leaders.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Ambassador.title: NCR Ambassador
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Barracks_Baby.descript: Like many other children within the NCR, [PAWN_nameDef] was conceived, born, and raised within the compound of an NCR army outpost. [PAWN_pronoun] enjoyed a rather practical education and was trained in the use of firearms from a young age.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Barracks_Baby.title: Barracks Baby
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Brahmin_Baron.descript: : As the NCR grew in size, several former ranchers managed to monopolize much of the livestock, usually in the form of the ubiquitous brahmin, which gave them considerable power and influence within the young nation. [PAWN_nameDef] was one of those so-called brahmin barons. [PAWN_pronoun] acquired much of [PAWN_possessive] extensive holdings through questionable means, which usually involved bribery or blackmail.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Brahmin_Baron.title: NCR Brahmin Baron
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Citizen.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was a citizen of the New California Republic. [PAWN_pronoun] upheld the values of the republic, such as democracy, personal liberty and the rule of law and wishes to spread them throughout the wasteland.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Citizen.title: NCR Citizen
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Crimson_Caravan_Worker: [PAWN_nameDef] worked for the Crimson Caravan, one of the oldest and largest caravan outfits in the New California Republic. While the pay was good, [PAWN_pronoun] always dreamed of starting [PAWN_possessive] own caravan.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Disgruntled_Veteran.de: [PAWN_nameDef] served time in the NCR military, fighting for the values of the republic, or so [PAWN_pronoun] initially thought… With time, [PAWN_nameDef] became disillusioned, as [PAWN_pronoun] realized that the NCR often acted out of material interests. After [PAWN_possessive] service ended, [PAWN_pronoun] didn’t reenlist, as [PAWN_pronoun] no longer wanted to participate in what [PAWN_pronoun] thought to be imperialism.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Doctor_a.description: [PAWN_nameDef] didn’t expect medicine to be this messy. When [PAWN_pronoun] signed up with the NCR, [PAWN_possessive] dreams were of clean clinics, careful surgeries, and patients who had a real chance at survival. Instead, [PAWN_pronoun] spent [PAWN_possessive] career digging bullets out of screaming soldiers, using whatever tools were on hand to keep them alive for just one more fight. The Republic’s wars never end, and neither does [PAWN_possessive] work.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Doctor_a.title: NCR Doctor
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Doctor_b.description: To [PAWN_nameDef], the Republic isn’t about war, it’s about saving lives. [PAWN_pronoun] treats NCR soldiers, wastelanders, even captured enemies, because life is too precious to waste on petty politics. Many of [PAWN_possessive] fellow officers call [PAWN_pronoun] soft, but [PAWN_pronoun] believes that if the Republic truly stands for something, it should be compassion.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Doctor_b.title: NCR Doctor
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Doctor_c.description: Before the NCR, [PAWN_nameDef] was a Scribe of the Brotherhood of Steel, trained in medical science. But [PAWN_pronoun] saw firsthand how the Brotherhood hoarded technology, refusing to use it to help the wasteland. That wasn’t good enough. [PAWN_pronoun] fled to the NCR, offering [PAWN_possessive] expertise to a faction that actually helped people. Some troopers still don’t trust [PAWN_pronoun], but [PAWN_possessive] skills have kept them alive too many times to ignore.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Doctor_c.title: NCR Doctor
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Doctor_d.description: Before the NCR, before the Republic’s war machine, [PAWN_nameDef] was just another wasteland doctor, moving from settlement to settlement, patching up whoever could pay. Eventually, the NCR made [PAWN_pronoun] an offer, regular supplies, security, and a real place to work. It wasn’t a hard choice. But even now, [PAWN_pronoun] sometimes wonders if the Republic is really any different from the warlords [PAWN_pronoun] used to avoid.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Doctor_d.title: NCR Doctor
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Enthusiastic_Recruit.d: Since the day [PAWN_pronoun] could hold a BB gun, [PAWN_nameDef] wanted to join the NCR Armed Forces. Much to the confusion of [PAWN_possessive] peers and parents [PAWN_nameDef] shunned playing with the other children, as well as book learning and preferred to sharpen [PAWN_possessive] aim instead.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Farmhand.description: A substantial part of the Republic’s power came from its vast amounts of arable land. [PAWN_nameDef] worked as a farmland for most of [PAWN_possessive] life, traveling from farm to farm to help in harvesting the crops that would ensure the NCR’s stability and prosperity.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Farmhand.title: NCR Farmhand
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Field_Medic.descriptio: While [PAWN_pronoun] was a member of the NCR military, [PAWN_nameDef] didn’t join it to end lives, but to save them. [PAWN_nameDef] was a skilled field medic and saved many soldiers from certain death. However, [PAWN_pronoun] was not a very skilled fighter and relied on others when in battle.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Field_Medic.title: NCR Field Medic
[PAWN_nameDef] was once part of the New California Republic Congress. [PAWN_pronoun] was a good, honest politician and looked after the wellbeing of all [PAWN_possessive] constituents. Unfortunately, [PAWN_pronoun] made some powerful enemies, namely some rich and influential brahmin barons, who eventually managed to fabricate [PAWN_nameDef]’s downfall and deselection. This experience left [PAWN_objective] frustrated and angry, enough to make [PAWN_objective] leave [PAWN_possessive] town forever.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Gun_Runner_Artisan.des: [PAWN_nameDef] worked as an artisan for the Gun Runners, the biggest weapon manufacturer within the New California Republic. The Gun Runners protected their facilities with infamous jealousy and [PAWN_nameDef] was not an exception. Because of this [PAWN_pronoun] has had trouble trusting others, since [PAWN_pronoun] feared they were after [PAWN_possessive] weapon schematics.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Heavy_Trooper_a.descri: [PAWN_nameDef] never wanted to be a soldier, but [PAWN_pronoun] always wanted to protect people. In the Heavy Trooper corps, [PAWN_pronoun] found a way to do both. [PAWN_pronoun] has learned to use [PAWN_possessive] salvaged armor as a shield for [PAWN_possessive] squadmates, standing firm under fire so others can fight another day. The Republic may not be perfect, but as long as [PAWN_pronoun] is standing, [PAWN_pronoun] will keep it safe.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Heavy_Trooper_a.title: NCR Heavy Trooper
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Heavy_Trooper_b.descri: Heavy Troopers were trained to hold the line, but [PAWN_nameDef] had a different idea, break the enemy’s line. Given salvaged power armor, [PAWN_possessive] first instinct was to charge, bringing close-range devastation to anyone too slow to run. [PAWN_pronoun] relishes the feeling of wading into a battlefield, taking shots that would kill normal troopers and coming out with nothing but dents. Some call [PAWN_pronoun] reckless. [PAWN_possessive] superiors just call [PAWN_pronoun] effective.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Heavy_Trooper_b.title: NCR Heavy Trooper
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Heavy_Trooper_c.descri: [PAWN_nameDef] wasn’t supposed to last. Heavy Trooper training is brutal, and most wash out. But through sheer stubbornness, [PAWN_pronoun] endured, refusing to quit no matter how many times [PAWN_possessive] body failed. Years later, [PAWN_possessive] original trainers are either dead or retired, and [PAWN_pronoun] is still here, still standing. The NCR may be flawed, but it’s given [PAWN_pronoun] a purpose, and [PAWN_pronoun] won’t let it go.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Heavy_Trooper_c.title: NCR Heavy Trooper
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Heavy_Trooper_d.descri: [PAWN_nameDef] should be dead by now. The NCR has sent [PAWN_pronoun] into some of the worst hellholes on the frontlines, and yet [PAWN_pronoun] keeps coming back. Some say it’s the armor. Some say it’s sheer luck. Whatever it is, [PAWN_possessive] reputation as an unkillable soldier has made [PAWN_pronoun] both feared and respected. No matter how many battles come, [PAWN_pronoun] will fight until there’s nothing left.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Heavy_Trooper_d.title: NCR Heavy Trooper
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Heavy_Trooper_e.descri: Heavy Troopers don’t just fight the Legion, they also enforce NCR rule in the frontier. When diplomacy fails, heavies move in. [PAWN_nameDef] has crushed uprisings, disarmed dissidents, and put down anyone who thought they could defy the Republic. Some NCR officers hesitate to use force. [PAWN_pronoun] does not. The Republic’s laws are meaningless if people don’t fear breaking them.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Heavy_Trooper_e.title: NCR Heavy Trooper
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Khan_Survivor.descript: As a child [PAWN_nameDef] survived the massacre at Bitter Springs, unlike [PAWN_possessive] friends and family. [PAWN_pronoun] was first taken into custody and later adopted by NCR citizens who, much like most of the NCR military, avoided talking with [PAWN_objective] about this dark chapter in the republic’s history. Instead, [PAWN_nameDef] learned to express [PAWN_objective]self artistically to cope with [PAWN_possessive] trauma.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Khan_Survivor.title: Khan Survivor
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Manufacturers_Apprenti: The many conflicts along the borders of the NCR led to a constant need for new equipment and gear for the Republic’s Army. The manufactory in which [PAWN_nameDef] was raised profited immensely from this near constant state of war. Thus, [PAWN_possessive] parents were wealthy enough to afford a high standard of living, by post-apocalyptic standards.
As a fledgling nation, the NCR’s infrastructure was still largely underdeveloped. The many construction sites offered employment to countless migrant workers, moving from one place and project to another. [PAWN_nameDef] was born into this lifestyle. While [PAWN_pronoun] received little formal education, [PAWN_possessive] hardy upbringing meant [PAWN_pronoun] was better prepared for the life full of privations the wasteland offered than most other children.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Military_Police_Troope: [PAWN_nameDef] was a member of the NCR military police. It was [PAWN_possessive] duty to ensure discipline among the troops, as well as to punish those that violated the law. In turn, it was expected of [PAWN_objective] to lead by example. [PAWN_nameDef] was an exemplary soldier, disciplined and loyal, but never really had the creativity and leadership qualities, that were necessary to rise up in rank.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Navarro_Survivor.descr: Until the sacking of Navarro at the hands of the NCR military, [PAWN_nameDef] was a member of the Enclave. [PAWN_pronoun] was one of the few survivors of that battle. Separated from [PAWN_possessive] former comrades, [PAWN_nameDef] decided to lay low to escape the wrath of the NCR, taking work wherever [PAWN_pronoun] could find it and never staying long at one location. The skills [PAWN_nameDef] learned during [PAWN_possessive] time with the enclave came in handy with many of the jobs [PAWN_pronoun] took over the years.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Navarro_Survivor.title: Navarro Survivor
Navarro Survivor
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Ne_er_do_well.descript: [PAWN_nameDef] was a street urchin in one of the many settlements of the NCR. It became apparent early on in [PAWN_possessive] life, that [PAWN_pronoun] adapt in getting [PAWN_objective]self into trouble and many thought it would be only a matter of time until [PAWN_pronoun]’d end up in jail.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Ne_er_do_well.title: Ne'er do well
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Officer.description: Upon reaching adulthood, [PAWN_nameDef] joined the NCR armed forces and was awarded the rank of an officer due to the political appointments of his close relatives. Despite lacking the combat experience or tactical skills of more veteran and hardend officers, [PAWN_pronoun] is like by [PAWN_possessive] troops, knows how to talk to and persuade other officers and politicians, and is eager to learn.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Officer.title: NCR Officer
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Officer_a.description: [PAWN_nameDef] never wanted to be an officer. In the early days of [PAWN_possessive] enlistment, [PAWN_pronoun] was just another grunt in the trenches, dodging bullets and barely surviving Legion ambushes. But when [PAWN_possessive] commanding officer fell, [PAWN_pronoun] took charge out of necessity. The Republic rewarded [PAWN_pronoun] with a battlefield commission, and ever since, [PAWN_pronoun] has been leading from the front—whether [PAWN_pronoun] likes it or not.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Officer_a.title: NCR Officer
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Officer_b.description: Diplomacy is for politicians, officers exist to win wars. [PAWN_nameDef] has no patience for soft-spoken negotiators or bureaucrats who slow the Republic’s expansion. The NCR is meant to conquer, to bring order to lawless wastes. Every battle is an opportunity to expand the Republic’s reach, and [PAWN_pronoun] will not let weak-willed fools stand in the way.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Officer_b.title: NCR Officer
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Officer_c.description: The NCR preaches justice and democracy, but out here in the wasteland, real power comes from who you know and what you control. [PAWN_nameDef] learned early on that the best way to thrive in the Republic wasn’t through valor but through favors, bribes, and well-placed threats. The rank of officer was just another step toward controlling the Republic’s real power structure.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Officer_c.title: NCR Officer
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Officer_d.description: [PAWN_nameDef] didn’t join the NCR to fight, [PAWN_pronoun] joined to rise. Every battle, every victory, every strategic decision is just a stepping stone toward something greater. War is just another game of politics, and [PAWN_pronoun] intends to win.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Officer_d.title: NCR Officer
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Officer_e.description: The Brotherhood doesn’t take prisoners. When [PAWN_nameDef] led a patrol into contested NCR territory, the mission was supposed to be a simple scouting operation. Instead, it turned into a massacre. [PAWN_pronoun] barely escaped, while [PAWN_possessive] entire unit was wiped out by a single squad of Brotherhood Knights. The NCR may have won the war against them, but [PAWN_pronoun] knows better, the Brotherhood isn’t gone, just hiding, waiting to strike again. And [PAWN_pronoun] will be waiting too.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Officer_e.title: NCR Officer
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Officer_Offspring.desc: [PAWN_nameDef] was a child of an officer within the NCR Armed Forces. While [PAWN_pronoun] received an education only a privileged few enjoyed, [PAWN_pronoun] was exposed to a lot of pressure due to the high expectations [PAWN_possessive] parents had.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Pioneer.description: [PAWN_nameDef] lived in the NCR but moved to its border regions to found a new settlement. [PAWN_pronoun] has all the necessary skills to build a new community in the wasteland.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Pioneer.title: NCR Pioneer
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Plantation_Child.descr: The NCR was dotted with plantations, where irrigation and lack of radiation allowed the cultivation of crops. There the republic produced a significant amount of food in the form of hardy plants, like corn or tatos, to feed its hungry settlements. [PAWN_nameDef] was born on one of these plantations and learned how to tend the crops from a very young age. [PAWN_possessive] relatively peaceful upbringing meant that [PAWN_pronoun] lacked many survival skills.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Plantation_Child.title: Plantation Child
Plantation Child
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Private.description: Upon reaching adulthood, [PAWN_nameDef] joined the NCR armed forces and was awarded the rank of private. Despite lacking the combat experience of more senior soldiers, [PAWN_pronoun] is decent with a gun, knows how to build basic defensive structures, like barricades or sandbags and knows a thing or two about first aid.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Private.title: NCR Private
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Quarry_worker.descript: The NCR had developed a habit of building fortifications and bunkers on strategic positions along their borders. To do that however, they needed large quantities of stone to make cement, which in turn needed to be mined first. [PAWN_nameDef] worked in one of the many quarries of within and without NCR territory. It was hard, backbreaking work but the pay was good and that was all that mattered to [PAWN_pronoun].
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Quarry_worker.title: Quarry worker
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Quartermaster.descript: [PAWN_nameDef] was a quartermaster in the NCR military. [PAWN_pronoun] was tasked with keeping track of provisions for the soldiers. As the NCR military was notoriously undersupplied [PAWN_pronoun] got used to negotiate over every piece of equipment, both with fellow members of the NCR, as well as with outsiders who often were a reliable source of supplies.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Quartermaster.title: NCR Quartermaster
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Raised_by_Followers.de: [PAWN_nameDef] was raised within an outpost of the Followers of the Apocalypse. The Followers did their best to shelter [PAWN_objective] from the dangers of the wasteland and educated [PAWN_objective] in many academic disciplines, from medicine to sociology. While [PAWN_nameDef] became an expert on many fields, the humanistic ideals of [PAWN_possessive] foster parents made [PAWN_objective] a staunch pacifist.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Rancher.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was a rancher, tending and caring for brahmin and bighorners. [PAWN_pronoun] was great with animals, but not so much with people, preferring the company of livestock to other humans and the solitude of the plains to the bustle of settlements. [PAWN_pronoun] also had some combat abilities, gained from defending [PAWN_possessive] herds from rustlers.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Rancher.title: NCR Rancher
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Ranger.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was part of the elite fighting force of the NCR, the rangers. As such [PAWN_pronoun] excels in combat and has quite a few other survival skills, owed to years of service in and around the Mojave.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Ranger.title: NCR Ranger
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Rangers_Child.descript: [PAWN_nameDef] was born as a child of an NCR ranger. [PAWN_pronoun] spent most of [PAWN_possessive] childhood within a remote ranger outpost somewhere in the hinterlands of the NCR territory. [PAWN_nameDef] was a skilled survivalist indeed, but never got used to large crowds of people due to the isolated location of [PAWN_possessive] birthplace.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Rangers_Child.title: Ranger’s Child
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Re_educated_Raider_Kid: [PAWN_nameDef] was born into a raider gang, which had its territories within the NCR. During the NCR-Raider-War, in which most raider groups in the republic were either wiped out or driven out, [PAWN_pronoun] was captured. The republic put much effort into the re-education of [PAWN_nameDef], but [PAWN_pronoun] never managed to shed all [PAWN_pronoun] had learned as a raider and remained somewhat untamed and savage.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Recon_Trooper.descript: As a member of a recon squad, [PAWN_nameDef] was a skilled sniper. When not on reconnaissance missions, [PAWN_possessive] squad was usually deployed to take out enemy commanders or leaders, often tipping the scale in the ongoing conflict between the NCR and whatever foe tried to stand in their way.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Recon_Trooper.title: NCR Recon Trooper
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Republic_Born.descript: [PAWN_nameDef] was born in the New California Republic. Unlike many children in other parts of the wasteland, [PAWN_pronoun] received basic education at a school, where [PAWN_pronoun] learned to write and read, as well as the history of the republic. [PAWN_possessive] parents were proud of their NCR origin and taught [PAWN_objective] to uphold the republic’s values.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Republic_Born.title: Republic Born
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Republic_Migrant.descr: [PAWN_nameDef] parents moved to the NCR while [PAWN_pronoun] was a small child, seeking a better life within the republic. At first, they had some troubles adapting to their new surroundings and its society but eventually the family learned to value life in the NCR, albeit they never quite grew accustomed to paying taxes.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Republic_Migrant.title: Republic Migrant
Republic Migrant
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Road_Worker.descriptio: As the NCR grew, so did its need for roads which connected its towns and settlements. [PAWN_nameDef] made a living as a road worker, labouring under the remorseless sun of the wasteland, to ensure that the caravans made it to all settlements that needed their goods. While [PAWN_pronoun] was quite strong due to [PAWN_possessive] backbreaking work, [PAWN_pronoun] never was much of a book learner.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Road_Worker.title: NCR Road Worker
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Scientist_a.descriptio: [PAWN_nameDef] was once a scientist for the Enclave, part of a classified research division dedicated to advancing pre-war technology. But the deeper [PAWN_pronoun] dug, the clearer it became, the Enclave wasn’t saving America. It was dooming it all over again. When the NCR pushed into Enclave held territory, [PAWN_pronoun] saw a chance to escape and took it. Now, [PAWN_pronoun] uses [PAWN_possessive] knowledge to help the Republic, though some still don’t trust a former Enclave scientist.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Scientist_a.title: NCR Scientist
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Scientist_b.descriptio: The NCR doesn’t win wars just by outnumbering the enemy, it wins with firepower. That’s where [PAWN_nameDef] comes in. [PAWN_pronoun] has spent [PAWN_possessive] entire career developing better guns, better explosives, and deadlier ways to ensure the Republic’s dominance. Some people call [PAWN_pronoun] work ruthless, but they don’t complain when NCR Heavy Troopers melt through enemy lines with plasma weaponry.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Scientist_b.title: NCR Scientist
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Scientist_c.descriptio: The wasteland is a hostile, mutating place, and [PAWN_nameDef] has spent [PAWN_possessive] life studying it. From FEV strains to aggressive radscorpions, [PAWN_pronoun] sees the world as a testbed for scientific progress. The Republic frowns on [PAWN_possessive] experiments, but [PAWN_pronoun] knows the strongest survive, and science must be free to push boundaries.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Scientist_c.title: NCR Scientist
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Scientist_d.descriptio: The Brotherhood of Steel sees technology as a treasure to be hoarded. [PAWN_nameDef] saw it as a tool for progress. When the NCR went to war with the Brotherhood, [PAWN_pronoun] made a choice, defect and share [PAWN_possessive] knowledge with the Republic. Now, [PAWN_pronoun] works in NCR research labs, studying power armor, energy weapons, and other Brotherhood secrets.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Scientist_d.title: NCR Scientist
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Scientist_e.descriptio: [PAWN_nameDef] was born in a vault, one of the few still operating when the NCR came knocking. The Republic took control, offering [PAWN_pronoun] a chance to put [PAWN_possessive] skills to use for something greater. [PAWN_pronoun] accepted, eager to study the wasteland firsthand. The surface world is dangerous, unpredictable, and full of scientific wonders.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Scientist_e.title: NCR Scientist
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Settled_Tribal.descrip: [PAWN_nameDef] was born into a tribal society, which dwelled within NCR territory. At some point during [PAWN_possessive] childhood the tribe was pacified and integrated into the Republic’s society. While not all members of the tribe looked favourably upon the new lifestyle forced upon them, [PAWN_nameDef] was fascinated by the possibilities of the “modern” life of an NCR-citizen.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Settled_Tribal.title: Settled Tribal
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Sheriff.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was elected as a Sheriff in a small town that was part of the New California Republic. [PAWN_pronoun] couldn’t enforce law and order as independently as the Sheriffs of non-republic settlements, but still held a lot of influence in [PAWN_possessive] town and most of its inhabitants looked up to [PAWN_objective].
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Sheriff.title: NCR Sheriff
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Squatter.description: [PAWN_nameDef] was a squatter, following the NCR military in recently incorporated areas to find a place to settle down and build a new life. Many inhabitants of these areas looked unfavourably upon these squatters and so [PAWN_pronoun] had to watch out for repercussions by angry locals.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Squatter.title: NCR Squatter
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Tax_Collector.descript: While most people appreciated the services and protection the NCR offered to its citizens, the taxes that were levied to pay for these things were considerably less popular. The people who came to collect these taxes in the name of the Californian government were equally undesired, but [PAWN_nameDef] took pride in [PAWN_possessive] work anyway. [PAWN_pronoun] wasn’t exactly a fighter and relied on either words or an armed escort to deal with those unwilling to pay their share, as well as with those few ne'er-do-wells who were brave or foolish enough to try stealing [PAWN_possessive] coffers.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Tax_Collector.title: NCR Tax Collector
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Trooper_a.description: When [PAWN_nameDef] enlisted in the NCR Army, [PAWN_pronoun] truly believed in its mission: order, democracy, and justice for the wasteland. But years on the frontlines have tested that faith. Corrupt officers, supply shortages, and brutal guerilla warfare against the Legion have made [PAWN_pronoun] question if the Republic can really bring peace to the wasteland. Still, [PAWN_possessive] duty isn’t done yet. If the NCR is flawed, then [PAWN_nameDef] will fight to make it better.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Trooper_a.title: NCR Trooper
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Trooper_b.description: [PAWN_nameDef] barely made it through training, and everyone knew it. Out of shape, undisciplined, and barely able to follow orders, [PAWN_pronoun] should have been kicked out. But the NCR needed bodies, and so [PAWN_pronoun] was given a rifle and sent to the front. Now, [PAWN_pronoun] spends more time avoiding patrols than fighting, hoping to live long enough to get a discharge preferably with all [PAWN_possessive] limbs intact.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Trooper_b.title: NCR Trooper
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Trooper_c.description: [PAWN_nameDef] didn’t join for patriotism. [PAWN_pronoun] joined because it was a paycheck. The NCR Army provides food, gear, and a chance to loot whatever’s left on the battlefield. Sure, the work is dangerous, but [PAWN_pronoun] knows that war isn’t about ideals, it’s about survival. And if the Republic ever stops paying, well… there’s always someone else willing to hire a soldier with experience.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Trooper_c.title: NCR Trooper
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Trooper_d.description: Before the NCR, [PAWN_nameDef] lived by raider code, take what you want, kill who you need, and never look back. But raider gangs don’t last forever, and when [PAWN_possessive] crew was wiped out by an NCR patrol, [PAWN_pronoun] had two choices: a bullet or a uniform. The army’s discipline was suffocating at first, but over time, [PAWN_pronoun] found [PAWN_possessive] skills had a place here. Killing was killing, after all, did it really matter who?
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Trooper_d.title: NCR Trooper
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Trooper_e.description: The first time [PAWN_nameDef] saw an NCR soldier, [PAWN_pronoun] thought [PAWN_pronoun] was about to die. Instead, they cut [PAWN_pronoun] free. Born into Legion slavery, [PAWN_possessive] entire life had been toil and punishment. The idea of freedom was as foreign as the land beyond [PAWN_possessive] camp. When the NCR offered a new life in the army, [PAWN_pronoun] accepted—partly out of gratitude, partly because [PAWN_pronoun] didn’t know how to live any other way.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Trooper_e.title: NCR Trooper
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Trooper_f.description: [PAWN_nameDef] joined the NCR not as a fighter, but as an engineer, working to maintain vehicles, fortifications, and energy grids. But when battles started turning against the Republic, every warm body was given a rifle including [PAWN_pronoun]. Now, [PAWN_pronoun] fights alongside the same soldiers [PAWN_pronoun] used to support, struggling to balance the cold logic of engineering with the chaotic brutality of war.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Trooper_f.title: NCR Trooper
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Van_Graff_Thug.descrip: While the Van Graff family tried to appear as legitimate businesspeople, they rather resembled a crime syndicate. [PAWN_nameDef] worked as a thug for the Van Graffs for quite some time and received extensive training with energy weapons, of which the Van Graff family was a major distributor. While [PAWN_pronoun] was not quite as unscrupulous as [PAWN_possessive] employers, [PAWN_pronoun] was still willing to take care of many tasks other, more moral people wouldn’t have dared to do.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Van_Graff_Thug.title: Van Graff Thug
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCRCF_Inmate.description: With the return of law and order in NCR territory, correctional facilities, or prisons as they were usually called, made a comeback too. [PAWN_nameDef] managed to get into trouble once too often and eventually ended up in front of a judge. [PAWN_pronoun] was imprisoned for some time but was eventually released. While it wasn’t exactly an enjoyable experience, [PAWN_nameDef] learned a set of new skills in prison, many of which have proven to be useful even when not behind bars.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCRCF_Inmate.title: NCRCF Inmate
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Deserter_Colonel_A.bas: Once a decorated NCR colonel, he now leads deserters with ruthless efficiency, carving out territory in the wasteland.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Deserter_Colonel_B.bas: Renowned for his battlefield tactics, this colonel was exiled for insubordination and now commands a rogue army.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Deserter_Colonel_C.bas: With a vision for a new order, this colonel deserted the NCR to become a feared warlord among the wasteland's outcasts.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Deserter_Colonel_D.bas: Once respected, this colonel's fall from grace led him to gather deserters and forge a new path in the wasteland.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Deserter_Colonel_E.bas: Leading with experience and ambition, this colonel deserted to command mercenaries and carve out his own legacy.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Deserter_Officer_A.bas: Once an NCR officer, he used his rank for personal gain until his crimes were discovered. Now he leads deserters in the wasteland.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Deserter_Officer_B.bas: Frustrated by the NCR's poor leadership, this officer deserted to put his tactical skills to better use among the rogue ranks.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Deserter_Officer_C.bas: Banished for questioning orders, this commander now leads deserters in their fight against former allies and enemies alike.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Deserter_Officer_D.bas: Skilled in negotiation, this officer deserted to broker deals and alliances for the survival of his new faction.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Deserter_Officer_E.bas: Driven by revenge against the NCR, this captain deserted to lead attacks on his former comrades and claim his own justice.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Deserter_Trooper_A.bas: Once a loyal NCR trooper, he grew weary of endless campaigns and corrupt officers. Now he fights for survival among fellow deserters.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Deserter_Trooper_B.bas: Injured in battle and abandoned by command, this trooper deserted to escape a system that no longer cared for its own.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Deserter_Trooper_C.bas: Skilled at navigating the wasteland, this scout left the NCR to use his talents for his own survival and profit.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Deserter_Trooper_D.bas: Dreaming of riches, this trooper abandoned the NCR to become a gun for hire, selling his skills to the highest bidder.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Deserter_Trooper_E.bas: Once tasked with protecting NCR assets, a single mistake led to disgrace and desertion. Now he seeks redemption among outcasts.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Ambassador_Crocker_Adu: [PAWN_nameDef] served as the NCR's ambassador to New Vegas, tasked with maintaining diplomatic relations and advancing the Republic's interests in the Mojave. [PAWN_pronoun] became skilled at navigating complex political situations.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Ambassador_Crocker_Chi: [PAWN_nameDef] was raised in the NCR capital, watching [PAWN_possessive] parents navigate the complex world of post-war politics. [PAWN_pronoun] developed a keen understanding of negotiation and compromise.
[PAWN_nameDef] works as a botanist for the OSI, researching agricultural technologies to help feed the NCR's expanding population. [PAWN_pronoun] is passionate about the work but concerned about Hildern's reckless methods.
[PAWN_nameDef] grew up learning about botany and agriculture from [PAWN_possessive] parents. [PAWN_pronoun] was fascinated by how pre-war agricultural science could help feed the growing NCR population.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Captain_Curtis.baseDes: [PAWN_nameDef] managed supply operations at Camp McCarran, ensuring troops received necessary equipment. [PAWN_pronoun] became adept at procurement and 'creative' logistics to meet demands despite limited official resources.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Captain_Curtis.title: McCarran supply officer
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Captain_Curtis_Child.b: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up helping [PAWN_possessive] parents run a trading post, learning the value of supplies and efficient resource management from a young age.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Captain_Gilles_Adult.b: [PAWN_nameDef] commanded garrison forces at key NCR installations in the Mojave, balancing security needs with limited resources.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Captain_Gilles_Child.b: [PAWN_nameDef] was raised in Shady Sands, the capital of the NCR. [PAWN_pronoun] grew up surrounded by the Republic's ideals of democracy and expansion.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Captain_Parker_Adult.b: [PAWN_nameDef] served as an infantry captain in the NCR army, leading troops in numerous engagements against raiders, tribals, and hostile factions.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Captain_Parker_Child.b: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up on a farm in NCR territory, learning to work hard and shoot straight. [PAWN_pronoun] enlisted as soon as [PAWN_pronoun] was old enough.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Captain_Rodriguez.base: [PAWN_nameDef] commanded an NCR unit cut off from the Republic for over a decade. [PAWN_pronoun] maintained discipline and hope despite isolation, keeping [PAWN_possessive] troops together through sheer will and leadership.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Captain_Rodriguez_Chil: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up in the heart of NCR territory, learning early about duty, perseverance, and the challenges of maintaining order in the wasteland.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Chief_Hanlon_Adult.bas: [PAWN_nameDef] became a legendary NCR Ranger, serving with distinction for decades. As Ranger Chief, [PAWN_pronoun] led the defense of Hoover Dam during the First Battle, but grew disillusioned with the endless war.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Chief_Hanlon_Child.bas: [PAWN_nameDef] lost [PAWN_possessive] parents to raiders at a young age. [PAWN_pronoun] was taken in by the NCR Rangers, who became [PAWN_possessive] new family.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Colonel_Hsu_Adult.base: [PAWN_nameDef] served as commanding officer of Camp McCarran, managing thousands of troops in the Mojave. [PAWN_pronoun] earned respect for [PAWN_possessive] fairness and tactical competence.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Colonel_Hsu_Child.base: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up in the Hub, one of the NCR's core territories. [PAWN_pronoun] witnessed both the prosperity and challenges of the growing Republic, inspiring [PAWN_objective] to serve.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Colonel_Moore_Adult.ba: [PAWN_nameDef] became one of the NCR's most aggressive commanders, stationed at Hoover Dam. [PAWN_pronoun] believed in total victory and had no tolerance for weakness or compromise.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Colonel_Moore_Child.ba: [PAWN_nameDef] was raised with unwavering belief in the NCR's mission to bring order to the wasteland. [PAWN_pronoun] learned that strength and determination were the keys to survival.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Comm_Officer_Gaines_Ad: [PAWN_nameDef] coordinated military communications for NCR operations in the Mojave, managing multiple channels and ensuring efficient information flow.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Comm_Officer_Gaines_Ch: [PAWN_nameDef] attended NCR military academy, showing aptitude for communications and coordination that set [PAWN_objective] apart from other students.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Comm_Officer_Grant_Adu: [PAWN_nameDef] managed field communications for NCR units, setting up mobile radio stations and maintaining contact with command during operations.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Comm_Officer_Grant_Chi: [PAWN_nameDef] worked as a message runner between NCR settlements, learning the importance of clear and accurate communication.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Comm_Officer_Lenk_Adul: [PAWN_nameDef] managed communications for NCR forces in the Mojave, coordinating radio traffic and ensuring messages reached their destinations.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Comm_Officer_Lenk_Chil: [PAWN_nameDef] was fascinated by radio technology from a young age, tinkering with old equipment and learning how communications worked in the wasteland.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Comm_Officer_Pappas_Ad: [PAWN_nameDef] maintained and operated NCR radio equipment, ensuring communications stayed functional even in harsh wasteland conditions.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Comm_Officer_Pappas_Ch: [PAWN_nameDef] scavenged pre-war technology with [PAWN_possessive] family, learning how old communications equipment worked and how to repair it.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Comm_Officer_Richards_: [PAWN_nameDef] specialized in signal operations for the NCR military, managing secure communications and encryption protocols.
[PAWN_nameDef] learned signal communication from an NCR veteran, studying codes and radio procedures that would later serve [PAWN_objective] well.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Comm_Officer_Stepinac_: [PAWN_nameDef] managed maintenance and repair of NCR communications equipment, ensuring the vital radio network stayed operational.
[PAWN_nameDef] helped [PAWN_possessive] guardian maintain NCR communications equipment, learning the technical side of radio operations.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Comm_Officer_Stone_Adu: [PAWN_nameDef] supervised communications dispatch for NCR military units, managing emergency communications and coordinating rapid response.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Comm_Officer_Stone_Chi: [PAWN_nameDef] learned communications from [PAWN_possessive] parents, who operated telegraph and radio equipment for an NCR outpost. [PAWN_pronoun] grew up with the clicking of morse code.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Comm_Officer_Traci_Adu: [PAWN_nameDef] specialized in tactical communications, coordinating real-time battlefield communications for NCR forces during combat operations.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Comm_Officer_Traci_Chi: [PAWN_nameDef] trained as a dispatcher for NCR caravans, learning to coordinate movements and relay information effectively.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Corporal_Betsy_Adult.b: [PAWN_nameDef] served in 1st Recon's Alpha Squad as a spotter. After a traumatic encounter with Cook-Cook of the Fiends, [PAWN_pronoun] struggled but remained determined.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Corporal_Betsy_Child.b: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up on the edge of NCR territory, learning to handle threats from raiders and wildlife. [PAWN_pronoun] enlisted to help protect others.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Corporal_Hornsby_Adult: [PAWN_nameDef] served in the NCR Military Police at Camp McCarran, maintaining order and investigating crimes among the troops.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Corporal_Hornsby_Child: [PAWN_nameDef] attended an NCR military academy as a teenager, learning discipline and combat skills. [PAWN_pronoun] excelled at following orders.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Corporal_Mags_Adult.ba: [PAWN_nameDef] served in the 9th Platoon, nicknamed "The Misfits" for their poor performance. Despite this reputation, [PAWN_pronoun] remained loyal to the NCR.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Corporal_Mags_Child.ba: [PAWN_nameDef] had a difficult childhood, getting into trouble frequently. The NCR military offered [PAWN_objective] a chance at redemption and purpose.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Corporal_Sterling_Adul: [PAWN_nameDef] earned the right to wear Ranger patrol armor and trained under experienced Rangers, working to prove [PAWN_objective] worthy of full Ranger status.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Corporal_Sterling_Chil: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up idolizing the NCR Rangers and dreaming of joining their ranks. [PAWN_pronoun] trained constantly to prove [PAWN_objective] worthy.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Doctor_Keely_Adult.bas: [PAWN_nameDef] is an OSI researcher specializing in botany and ecology. [PAWN_pronoun] was sent to Vault 22 to recover agricultural data but became trapped by the vault's mutated plant life.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Doctor_Keely_Child.bas: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up in a vault that emphasized scientific education and environmental studies. [PAWN_pronoun] was fascinated by biology and ecology from a young age.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Doctor_Kilborn_Adult.b: [PAWN_nameDef] served as the senior medical officer at Camp Forlorn Hope, working alongside Medical Officer Richards. Together they treated countless wounded soldiers with minimal resources, their skills constantly tested by the brutal frontier conditions.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Doctor_Kilborn_Child.b: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up helping in a frontier clinic, learning medical techniques through hands-on experience. [PAWN_pronoun] witnessed countless preventable deaths that shaped [PAWN_possessive] dedication to medicine.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_First_Sergeant_Asher_A: [PAWN_nameDef] rose through the ranks to become First Sergeant of the legendary 1st Recon Battalion. [PAWN_pronoun] leads the NCR's most elite sniper unit with precision and discipline.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_First_Sergeant_Asher_C: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up hunting in the wastes, developing exceptional marksmanship skills from a young age. [PAWN_pronoun] could hit targets others couldn't even see.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_General_Oliver_Adult.b: [PAWN_nameDef] rose through the NCR military ranks to become General, taking command of the Hoover Dam campaign. Known as "General Wait-and-See," [PAWN_pronoun] favored cautious, defensive strategies.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_General_Oliver_Child.b: [PAWN_nameDef] was raised in a military family, learning discipline and strategy from an early age. [PAWN_pronoun] always knew [PAWN_pronoun] would follow in [PAWN_possessive] father's footsteps.
[PAWN_nameDef] served as a Military Police officer at Camp McCarran, investigating crimes and maintaining discipline among the troops.
[PAWN_nameDef] was raised by a former sheriff who instilled in [PAWN_objective] a strong sense of justice and order. [PAWN_pronoun] joined the NCR Military Police.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Lieutenant_Gorobets_Ad: [PAWN_nameDef] commanded a squad in the elite 1st Recon unit, specializing in long-range reconnaissance and precision marksmanship.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Lieutenant_Gorobets_Ch: [PAWN_nameDef] learned to track and shoot from [PAWN_possessive] father, a skilled wasteland hunter. These skills made [PAWN_objective] a natural fit for 1st Recon.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Lieutenant_Hayes_Adult: [PAWN_nameDef] led a platoon in the 1st Company, 5th Battalion, stationed at the vital Mojave Outpost. [PAWN_pronoun] kept morale up despite limited resources.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Lieutenant_Hayes_Child: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up moving from base to base as [PAWN_possessive] parents served in the NCR military. [PAWN_pronoun] knew the military life intimately.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Lieutenant_Markland_Ad: [PAWN_nameDef] commanded Camp Forlorn Hope, one of the NCR's most embattled positions. [PAWN_pronoun] struggled with supply shortages and low morale while trying to hold the line against Legion forces.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Lieutenant_Markland_Ch: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up in a military family and attended NCR officer training as a teenager. [PAWN_pronoun] showed natural leadership abilities and tactical thinking from an early age.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Lieutenant_Monroe_Adul: [PAWN_nameDef] commanded Camp Searchlight before it was irradiated by Legion forces. [PAWN_pronoun] barely survived the disaster that claimed most of [PAWN_possessive] troops.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Lieutenant_Monroe_Chil: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up in the harsh wasteland, learning to adapt and survive in a world still recovering from nuclear devastation. [PAWN_pronoun] saw military service as a path to stability.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Major_Dhatri_Adult.bas: [PAWN_nameDef] became a respected NCR major, known for [PAWN_possessive] competence and concern for [PAWN_possessive] troops. [PAWN_pronoun] led the effort to combat the Fiends threatening Camp McCarran.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Major_Dhatri_Child.bas: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up in Freeside before the NCR's arrival, witnessing the harsh reality of wasteland life. This shaped [PAWN_possessive] determination to bring order and safety to dangerous places.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Major_Kieran_Adult.bas: [PAWN_nameDef] rose through the ranks to become a major, leading troops in combat operations across the Mojave. [PAWN_pronoun] earned respect for [PAWN_possessive] tactical skill.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Major_Kieran_Child.bas: [PAWN_nameDef] was raised on a cattle ranch in NCR territory, learning discipline and hard work. [PAWN_pronoun] joined the military to see more of the world.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Major_Knight_Adult.bas: [PAWN_nameDef] specialized in supply logistics, ensuring NCR forces in the Mojave stayed equipped and fed. [PAWN_possessive] organizational skills proved invaluable.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Major_Knight_Child.bas: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up in one of the NCR's safe, prosperous cities. [PAWN_pronoun] enlisted to prove [PAWN_objective] could handle the dangers of the frontier.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Major_Polatli_Adult.ba: [PAWN_nameDef] became the quartermaster at Camp McCarran, responsible for managing the massive supply operations needed to support the NCR's Mojave campaign.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Major_Polatli_Child.ba: [PAWN_nameDef] traveled the wasteland with [PAWN_possessive] parents' caravan, learning to watch for danger and handle weapons at a young age.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Medical_Officer_Richar: [PAWN_nameDef] served as the chief medical officer at Camp Forlorn Hope, treating an endless stream of wounded soldiers with limited supplies. The constant casualties wore heavily on [PAWN_objective], but [PAWN_pronoun] refused to abandon [PAWN_possessive] post.
[PAWN_nameDef] learned medicine from a skilled wasteland doctor, developing [PAWN_possessive] healing abilities from a young age. [PAWN_pronoun] felt called to help others.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_President_Kimball_Adul: [PAWN_nameDef] rose to fame as a military leader in the Baja Campaign, earning the nickname "the Hero of the Mojave." [PAWN_pronoun] parlayed this military success into a political career, eventually becoming President of the NCR.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_President_Kimball_Chil: [PAWN_nameDef] grew up in the New California Republic as the son of prosperous ranchers. [PAWN_pronoun] learned the values of hard work, community, and the importance of strong leadership from an early age.
LanguageData.FCP_Backstory_NCR_Private_10_of_Spades_A: [PAWN_nameDef] earned promotion to Private First Class while stationed at Camp Golf. Known for [PAWN_possessive] card-playing skills and distinctive nickname, [PAWN_pronoun] proved reliable in combat despite [PAWN_possessive] laid-back demeanor.
